entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop studied in the field experiments in Niger

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where the field experiments were conducted

entities.name: WEST AFRICAN SAHEL
entities.description: The region where the crop production study took place

entities.name: SANDY PSAMMENTIC PALEUSTALF
entities.description: The specific soil type used in the experiments

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: The size of the seeds used for sowing, categorized as <1.95 mm, 1.95-2.12 mm, or >2.12 mm

entities.name: SOWING DEPTH
entities.description: The depth at which seeds were sown, tested at 1, 3, 5, or 7 cm

entities.name: SOWING METHOD
entities.description: The technique used for sowing, either in hills or drilling seeds

entities.name: SOWING IN HILLS
entities.description: A sowing method where seeds are placed in hills

entities.name: DRILLING SEEDS
entities.description: A sowing method where seeds are drilled

entities.name: PRE-SOWING TILLAGE
entities.description: Soil preparation methods including ploughing, ridging, sand fighting, or no-tillage

entities.name: PLOUGHING
entities.description: A specific pre-sowing tillage operation

entities.name: RIDGING
entities.description: A specific pre-sowing tillage operation involving the creation of ridges

entities.name: SAND FIGHTING
entities.description: A specific pre-sowing tillage operation used to combat sand

entities.name: NO-TILLAGE
entities.description: A pre-sowing condition where no tillage is performed

entities.name: FERTILIZER APPLICATION
entities.description: The application of 17 kg P and 40 kg N per hectare

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: A nutrient applied as part of the fertilizer

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A nutrient applied as part of the fertilizer

entities.name: CROP ESTABLISHMENT
entities.description: The process of the crop becoming established in the field

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The total agricultural production of the crop

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: STOVER YIELD
entities.description: The amount of stover produced per unit area

entities.name: PERCENTAGE EMERGENCE
entities.description: The percentage of seeds that successfully emerge from the soil

entities.name: SHOOT DW
entities.description: The dry weight of the crop shoots

entities.name: SECONDARY ROOTS
entities.description: The development and number of secondary roots

entities.name: CROP STAND SURVIVAL
entities.description: The rate at which the established crop stand survives

entities.name: WIND EROSION
entities.description: An environmental factor that adversely affects crop establishment and yield

relationships.description: Sowing depth of 3-5 cm resulted in the highest percentage emergence
Sowing depth of 3-5 cm resulted in the highest shoot dry weight
Sowing depth of 3-5 cm resulted in the most secondary roots
Seed size >2.12 mm resulted in higher emergence than smaller sizes
Seed size >2.12 mm resulted in higher shoot dry weight than smaller sizes
Seed size >2.12 mm resulted in more secondary roots than smaller sizes
Sowing in hills minimized the adverse effects of wind erosion
Sowing in hills led to better crop establishment than drilling seeds
Sowing in hills led to better crop stand survival than drilling seeds
Sowing in hills led to better yield than drilling seeds
Pre-sowing tillage increased initial stands and their survival
Application of P and N tripled grain yields
Ridging without prior tillage increased grain yields 2- to 3-fold
Ridging without prior tillage increased stover yields 2- to 3-fold
Combination of fertilizer and ridging resulted in 6-fold yield increases
Pearl millet was grown in field experiments in Niger
Pearl millet production was studied in the West African Sahel
Pearl millet was grown on sandy Psammentic Paleustalf soil
